title: Add color scheme options to Site Settings

requirements.text: Extend the backend SiteSettings record in main.mo to include a colorScheme field (Text) with a sensible default (e.g., 'default'). Update getSiteSettings and updateSiteSettings actor methods to include this field.

quotes: add color scheme options to site settings

acceptanceCriteria: SiteSettings record in main.mo includes a colorScheme Text field
updateSiteSettings accepts and persists the colorScheme value
Default value for colorScheme is a non-breaking empty string or named preset (e.g., 'default')

requirements.text: Update the SiteSettings TypeScript type in frontend/src/types.ts and the useQueries.ts hooks to include the colorScheme string field alongside existing SiteSettings fields.

quotes: add color scheme options to site settings

acceptanceCriteria: SiteSettings TypeScript interface includes colorScheme: string

requirements.text: Add a 'Color Scheme' subsection to the SiteSettingsSection component in the Admin page. Display a set of predefined color scheme options (e.g., 'Default (Ivory & Gold)', 'Rose Petal', 'Midnight Luxe', 'Sage & Cream') as selectable swatches or a radio/select control. Load the current value from getSiteSettings on mount, and save the selected scheme via the existing updateSiteSettings mutation. Display success/error toast notifications on save.

quotes: add color scheme options to site settings

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Color Scheme' subsection appears in the Site Settings tab of the Admin page
At least 4 predefined color scheme options are presented as selectable swatches or radio/select inputs
Each swatch visually previews the primary colors of that scheme
The currently saved color scheme is pre-selected when the section loads
Clicking 'Save' calls updateSiteSettings with the selected colorScheme value
A success toast appears on successful save; an error toast appears on failure

requirements.text: Apply the active color scheme site-wide by reading the colorScheme value from getSiteSettings on app load (in App.tsx or a top-level provider) and injecting the corresponding CSS custom property overrides onto the document root. Define CSS variable sets for each predefined scheme that override the existing brand color tokens in frontend/src/index.css (ivory, blush, gold, etc.).

quotes: add color scheme options to site settings

acceptanceCriteria: On app load, the active colorScheme is fetched from getSiteSettings
The corresponding CSS variable overrides are applied to :root or document.documentElement
Switching the color scheme in Admin Settings and saving immediately reflects the new palette on the storefront after a page reload or query invalidation
The 'Default' scheme preserves the existing ivory/blush/gold palette with no visual regression
Each alternative scheme applies visually distinct but cohesive palette changes across the navigation, hero banner, buttons, and product cards

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
The default color scheme must not cause any visual regression to the existing ivory/blush/gold theme
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor file (backend/main.mo)
nonGoals: Custom free-form color picker or hex input for arbitrary colors
Per-user or per-session color theme preferences
Dark mode toggle (separate concern from brand color schemes)
Modifying the Tailwind config at runtime
